author: @StefunnyStrange
text: I’m a black woman & I must say: Hollywood including DEI roles has actually made it WORSE for black actors, not better. In the 80s, 90s & 2000s, when a black person like Denzel, Will Smith, Angela Bassett, Morgan Freeman, etc were in a role, you could tell that they deserved to be there because they were extremely talented & fit the role perfectly. We were impressed with these black actors because we knew the casting was merit-based & that their success was well-deserved. It was special. That’s why people were star struck by them & why they gained so many fans. Now that we know these studios are picking based on identity & not merit, black actors rarely impress me anymore. Because in the back of my mind, even when I’m trying to block out negative thoughts so that I can enjoy the film, I know deep down that the actor/actress is only there for pity & to check off a box. That’s why you’re not seeing many new black stars emerge anymore who become household names, despite the fact that there are a lot more black actors now. That & due to the fact that most movies & shows these days are complete ASS.

replies.text:
@ComposedSage75 Fans are calling out many things. But not all of them are true.
I see people complaining about bad writing, which is hilarious because we've seen infamously bad written films becoming hits in the past.. 20 years? 
I see people whining about Disney live-action remakes, and yet many of those films were box office successes. Hypocrisy much?
Then there's the anti-woke mob, which is the dumbest group among fandoms, partially driven by politics, partially brainwashed by youtube grifters (like Nerdrotic).
There's fair criticism, and there's media illiteracy. All those I mentioned fall into the second category.

replies.author: @ComposedSage75
replies.text: ⁠@arthurshelby4010  the point I’m making isn’t whether or not there’s some successes in these IPs. The main issue is the fan’s ability to call out and criticize the direction the ones in charge want these franchises to go. There’s no brainwashing in that aspect because that would imply they’re being made to believe one thing over another despite seeing for themselves how these movies and shows are portrayed. There’s nothing wrong with being genuinely critical of any of these IPs because not every thing these places produce is going to be good or well received from others just because it has a popular name on it. That’s why dialogue is important despite how people feel about it.

replies.author: @arthurshelby4010
replies.text:
@ComposedSage75 Dialogue is important indeed. Blind hate isn't. 
True and sincere fans are passionate, they debate over their beloved franchises. Obsession is another thing entirely.
I loved Star Wars all my life, and yes I'm disappointed to see the mistakes Lucasfilm made in recent years. But that didn't turn me into a raging troll going all out and insulting Kathleen Kennedy, or the actors, directors, and showrunners just because some films and shows were mediocre. 
Also, it's naive to believe that Kennedy is the source of all evil, and that replacing her will magically restore Star Wars' glory. Or to think that erasing female-centric films/shows in Marvel will fix MCU.

replies.author: @ComposedSage75
replies.text: ⁠@arthurshelby4010 I disagree with the idea of this being “blind hate.” It’s moreso what I’ve already said of it being genuine criticisms from the evidence of them viewing the content shown. True and sincere fans are passionate but not obsessive to the point of assuming there’s some manner of hatred because of the individual(s) involved but the rhetoric those individuals champion from what those in charge have told them to for profit. Those are where the insults are coming from and just because they made these shows doesn’t mean they’re above any kind of criticism. It’s important to have distinctions between actions and rhetoric and that’s why I believe there’s always room for improvement and correction from these places.

author: @stevederp9801
text:
I watched an interview with Jackie mason 15 years ago that made me understand this concept before it ever even got to this point. 

Basically Jackie grew up in old Hollywood and watched it evolve but he like Rodney dangerfield remained grounded in what entertainment and show business really is, it’s basically a step above a circus or vaudeville. So he was asked why is it that there are so many far left people in Hollywood and his explanation has lived in my mind ever since that point. 

He basically explained that actors and actresses are people who make absurd amounts of money in such a short period of time. Unlike a businessman or an investor who had to work and risk losing their investments for years and then they finally make their millions of dollars. These actors and actresses are making millions of dollars for a few weeks of work and even the work they do is so minimal compared to the massive amount of people around them building sets, hanging lights, setting up cameras, doing make up and other various things that are all working class people and this one actor or actress makes a much money as all of them combined. 

So it’s about guilt. They know they don’t deserve that much money, they didn’t earn it and they feel bad about having so much money. They didn’t work for years and save up money to open a small business that finally takes off and then those business owners have 20 years of hard work and sacrifice to make the reward of their $20 million worth it. Actors and actresses were literally given that much money because they were good looking and they were marketable. They know they don’t deserve that money and so they identify with these socialist ideology where people like them should be forced to share their wealth with everyone around them. But ironically have you ever once seen George Clooney or Sean penn say they were going to take half their money and double all of the crews salary for the movie they make? No. They never once have done that. Have they ever advocated that all of the people who worked on the film receive royalties after the movie is released? No. See they’re called Champaign socialists and limousine liberals for a reason. They want to take other peoples money and give it to people. But they refuse to do it with their own vast resources.
